Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class BitmapDataArray

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.DataTypes
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Multi-bitplane bitmap storage for Apple IIe display rendering (560×192 pixels, 16 bitplanes).

public class BitmapDataArray
Inheritance
object
BitmapDataArray
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: This class provides a packed bitmap representation supporting up to 16 independent bitplanes (layers) per pixel. Each pixel position (x, y) has 16 boolean flags stored in a BitField16, allowing efficient storage of multi-channel bitmap data.

Apple IIe Dimensions: Fixed at 560×192 pixels to match Apple IIe video output:

  • Width (560): 280 hi-res pixels × 2 (horizontal doubling for square pixels), or 80 columns × 7 pixels per character
  • Height (192): 24 text rows × 8 scanlines per row
These dimensions are hardcoded constants to optimize performance and memory layout.

Bitplane Architecture: Each pixel has 16 independent boolean channels (bitplanes 0-15). In the current implementation:

  • Bitplane 0: Used for monochrome composite output (primary display)
  • Bitplanes 1-15: Reserved for future use (NTSC color separation, double hi-res channels, overlay effects, etc.)
This design enables future NTSC color artifact generation by separating color phases or implementing multi-layer rendering without changing the storage format.

Memory Layout: Stored as a flat array of 560 × 192 = 107,520 BitField16 values (215 KB). Pixels are stored row-major (left-to-right, top-to-bottom). Offset calculation: offset = x + (y × width).

Thread Safety: Not thread-safe. Multiple concurrent writes or simultaneous read/write operations require external synchronization. Typically used in a single-threaded rendering pipeline where one thread writes during frame generation and another reads during display.

Performance: Direct array access with bounds checking. All coordinate validation is performed before accessing the underlying array. For bulk operations, consider using GetRowDataSpan(int) to access entire rows efficiently.

Constructors

BitmapDataArray()

Initializes a new instance of the BitmapDataArray class.

public BitmapDataArray()

Remarks

Allocation: Allocates a flat array of 107,520 BitField16 values (215 KB). All pixels are initialized to zero (all bitplanes clear).

Performance: Array allocation is relatively fast (single allocation), but the 215 KB size should be considered when creating multiple instances. For double-buffering (front/back buffers), expect 430 KB total.

Properties

Height

Gets the height of the bitmap in scanlines (always 192).

public static int Height { get; }

Property Value

int

The fixed height of 192 scanlines.

Remarks

Static property to allow consumers (like FrameProvider) to query dimensions without needing an instance. The value is derived from the _height constant.

Width

Gets the width of the bitmap in pixels (always 560).

public static int Width { get; }

Property Value

int

The fixed width of 560 pixels.

Remarks

Static property to allow consumers (like FrameProvider) to query dimensions without needing an instance. The value is derived from the _width constant.

Methods

Clear()

Clears all pixels across all bitplanes (sets entire bitmap to zero).

public void Clear()

Remarks

Operation: Uses Clear(Array, int, int) to zero out the entire 107,520-element array. This is more efficient than looping through each pixel manually.

Result: After clearing, all pixels on all bitplanes are set to false (off). Equivalent to a blank screen.

Performance: Fast bulk operation optimized by the .NET runtime (typically memset at native level). Much faster than individual pixel operations.

Usage: Called at the start of each frame by ClearBuffer() to prepare for rendering.

ClearPixel(int, int, int)

Clears a pixel to off (false) on a specific bitplane.

public void ClearPixel(int x, int y, int bitplane)

Parameters

x int

X coordinate (0-559).

y int

Y coordinate (0-191).

bitplane int

Bitplane index (0-15).

Remarks

Operation: Finds the BitField16 at (x, y) and clears the specified bitplane to false (0). Other bitplanes at this pixel are unaffected.

Idempotent: Safe to call multiple times with the same coordinates - the pixel will remain clear. No error if the pixel is already clear.

Example: 

bitmap.ClearPixel(100, 50, 0);  // Clear pixel on bitplane 0 (monochrome)
bitmap.ClearPixel(100, 50, 1);  // Also clear on bitplane 1 (independent)

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if coordinates or bitplane are out of range.

GetBitplaneSpanForRow(int, int)

Gets a read-only span of boolean values for a single bitplane across an entire row.

public ReadOnlySpan<bool> GetBitplaneSpanForRow(int row, int bitplane)

Parameters

row int

Row index (0-191).

bitplane int

Bitplane index (0-15).

Returns

ReadOnlySpan<bool>

A ReadOnlySpan<T> of 560 boolean values representing the specified bitplane for all pixels in the row (left to right).

Remarks

Purpose: Efficient row-based access for rendering or analysis. Returns a contiguous view of one bitplane across an entire scanline.

Extraction: Iterates through the 560 BitField16 values for the row and extracts the specified bitplane bit from each, building a boolean array.

Performance: Allocates a 560-element boolean array (560 bytes on heap) and uses an optimized for-loop for extraction (more efficient than foreach). For zero-allocation scenarios, consider using GetRowDataSpan(int) for direct BitField16 access and extracting bits manually.

Usage Example: 

// Get all pixels on bitplane 0 for row 50
var pixels = bitmap.GetBitplaneSpanForRow(50, 0);
for (int x = 0; x < pixels.Length; x++)
{
    bool isLit = pixels[x];
    // Process pixel...
}

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if row is outside 0-191.

GetPixel(int, int, int)

Gets the state of a pixel on a specific bitplane.

public bool GetPixel(int x, int y, int bitplane)

Parameters

x int

X coordinate (0-559).

y int

Y coordinate (0-191).

bitplane int

Bitplane index (0-15).

Returns

bool

true if the pixel is set (on) on the specified bitplane; false if the pixel is clear (off).

Remarks

Operation: Finds the BitField16 at (x, y) and returns the boolean state of the specified bitplane.

Example: 

bitmap.SetPixel(100, 50, 0);
bool isSet = bitmap.GetPixel(100, 50, 0);  // true
bool otherPlane = bitmap.GetPixel(100, 50, 1);  // false (unless set separately)

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if coordinates or bitplane are out of range.

GetRowDataSpan(int)

Gets a read-only span of raw BitField16 values for an entire row.

public ReadOnlySpan<BitField16> GetRowDataSpan(int row)

Parameters

row int

Row index (0-191).

Returns

ReadOnlySpan<BitField16>

A ReadOnlySpan<T> of 560 BitField16 values representing all pixels in the row (all bitplanes included).

Remarks

Purpose: Zero-copy access to an entire scanline's raw data. Returns a span directly into the underlying _data array without allocation or copying.

Performance: Extremely efficient - creates a span view over existing memory. Ideal for bulk processing or when you need access to all bitplanes simultaneously.

Read-Only: The span is read-only to prevent accidental modification of the underlying array. For modifications, use SetPixel(int, int, int) or ClearPixel(int, int, int).

Usage Example: 

// Process all pixels in row 100 across all bitplanes
var rowData = bitmap.GetRowDataSpan(100);
for (int x = 0; x < rowData.Length; x++)
{
    BitField16 pixel = rowData[x];
    bool plane0 = pixel.GetBit(0);
    bool plane1 = pixel.GetBit(1);
    // Process multi-plane data...
}

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if row is outside 0-191.

SetPixel(int, int, int)

Sets a pixel to on (true) on a specific bitplane.

public void SetPixel(int x, int y, int bitplane)

Parameters

x int

X coordinate (0-559).

y int

Y coordinate (0-191).

bitplane int

Bitplane index (0-15).

Remarks

Operation: Finds the BitField16 at (x, y) and sets the specified bitplane to true (1). Other bitplanes at this pixel are unaffected.

Idempotent: Safe to call multiple times with the same coordinates - the pixel will remain set. No error if the pixel is already set.

Example: 

bitmap.SetPixel(100, 50, 0);  // Set pixel on bitplane 0 (monochrome)
bitmap.SetPixel(100, 50, 1);  // Also set on bitplane 1 (independent)

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if coordinates or bitplane are out of range.
